summary: Bake the talking-head camera onto the clean cut: a rect-keyframe crop trajectory rendered to pixels (GPU libplacebo, CPU-static fallback).

parity.note: The SHOT is JUDGEMENT and never crosses (detect_track / head_geometry / plan_shots / plan_punches decide it on the brain); only the rect keyframes it emits cross as params, and this op turns them into pixels. Two reasons it is not bit_exact. (1) The keyframes are a DENSE linear sampling (interp:linear, step 0.1s + every shot-cut and punch kink) of the camera planner's analytic COSINE ramp — linear-between-dense reproduces the cosine to a small residual (the planner measured 0.031→0.0036 max rect error at the punch kinks), so the trajectory itself carries a bounded approximation. (2) The primary render is GPU libplacebo on BOTH local and pod (the dev box and the rented pod both have Vulkan), so those two agree structurally — the crop filtergraph is pinned BYTE-EQUAL to the pod's rect renderer (podagent.render._gpu_crop) in the engine's parity suite — but a Vulkan-less box (driverless CI) falls back to a STATIC crop at the first keyframe, exactly as podagent.render._cpu_crop does in the final composite; that fallback is declared, not hidden. Tolerance for the GPU-vs-GPU path: PSNR >= 40 dB / SSIM >= 0.99 frame-to-frame is the intended bar (dominated by encoder quantisation, not geometry); UNPROVEN in a CPU-only environment with no GPU to run a real libplacebo render, so only the filtergraph STRINGS are pinned.

keyframes.description: The camera trajectory as DATA: per-frame crop rects in SOURCE fractions, emitted by head_trajectory.trajectory_for_keeps. Because cover is a uniform scale, a cover fraction IS a source fraction, so each rect applies straight to iw/ih (podagent.render.anim_expr, the SAME renderer the preview uses). Times are seconds on the segment clock.

rect.description: [x, y, w, h] crop window, each in [0,1].

interp.description: How adjacent keyframes are eased. The planner emits `linear` and relies on dense sampling (see parity note) rather than an analytic ease here — this stays an enum only so the same renderer serves the eased overlay presets.

encode_profile.description: Named quality tier, NOT encoder flags. head_dyn.mp4 is an INTERMEDIATE re-encoded downstream, so `final` runs a high-quality cq (14) to limit compounding generation loss; `preview` trades quality for the 540x960 proxy. The nvenc/libx264 ladder is tuning and stays in the handler (scripts/render_profile.py is the same SSOT pattern).
